content.text:
```json
[
  {
    "idea": "Electricity demand spike during extreme cold snap triggers utility sector rally",
    "family": "macro_input_pressure",
    "data": {
      "source": "EIA electricity demand weekly series through eia_electricity adapter",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "weekly",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "Winter temperature drops below -10°F in major grid regions cause residential heating demand surge, pushing electricity consumption to seasonal highs. Utilities benefit from higher utilization and pricing.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bullish",
      "sector": "XLU",
      "rationale": "Utilities are the direct beneficiary of sustained high electricity demand driven by heating needs."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "When weekly EIA total electricity demand increases 8% or more week-over-week AND temperature data shows 3+ consecutive days below -5°F in Northeast or Midwest",
      "exit": "After 10 trading days or when weekly demand falls back below 2% above baseline",
      "expected_frequency": "medium"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "weekly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 24,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "high",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Winter months (Dec-Feb) reliably produce multiple cold snaps with multi-week demand surges. EIA data updates weekly and extreme weather is seasonal and predictable."
  },
  {
    "idea": "Freight rail carload volume collapse signals industrial recession ahead",
    "family": "freight_logistics",
    "data": {
      "source": "AAR weekly carload traffic report via html_table scrape from Association of American Railroads public releases",
      "type": "scrape",
      "frequency": "weekly",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "Rail carloads (especially coal, metals, autos) decline sharply when manufacturing orders weaken. A 6%+ drop week-over-week signals demand destruction 2-4 weeks ahead and triggers industrial ETF selloff.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bearish",
      "sector": "XLI",
      "rationale": "Industrial production and carload traffic are tightly coupled; falling carloads precede earnings misses in construction, manufacturing, and transportation."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "When weekly total carloads decline 6% or more from prior week AND decline persists for 2 consecutive weeks",
      "exit": "After 8 trading days or when carloads stabilize and post 2 weeks of less than 2% decline",
      "expected_frequency": "medium"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "monthly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 24,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "high",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Carload cycles turn multiple times per year. Recessions, seasonal slowdowns, and trade shocks all produce multi-week drops. AAR data is reliable and published weekly."
  },
  {
    "idea": "Job opening postings collapse on employment board sites signals labor market cracking",
    "family": "labor_jobs",
    "data": {
      "source": "Indeed, ZipRecruiter, and Glassdoor job posting counts via rss_count or html_table weekly snapshot",
      "type": "scrape",
      "frequency": "weekly",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "Tech and consumer-facing job postings spike and crash in advance of earnings disappointments. A 12%+ week-over-week drop in open positions signals hiring pullback and upcoming layoffs.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bearish",
      "sector": "XLY",
      "rationale": "Job posting declines precede consumer discretionary weakness as hiring freezes reduce wage growth and job security confidence."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "When aggregate weekly job postings (tech + retail + hospitality) decline 12% or more from prior week",
      "exit": "After 7 trading days or when postings stabilize for 2 consecutive weeks above entry threshold",
      "expected_frequency": "medium"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "monthly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 12,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "high",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Job posting boards are highly volatile and responsive to economic cycles. Hiring cycles turn multiple times annually, producing 10-15%+ swings regularly."
  },
  {
    "idea": "Google Trends spike in 'bankruptcy filing' and 'debt consolidation' signals consumer default risk surge",
    "family": "consumer_stress",
    "data": {
      "source": "Google Trends weekly search volume for 'bankruptcy filing', 'debt consolidation', 'credit counseling' through google_trends adapter",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "weekly",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "Consumer searches for debt relief spike 4-8 weeks before credit card charge-offs rise and consumer discretionary earnings disappoint. Search surge signals financial desperation.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bearish",
      "sector": "XLY",
      "rationale": "Debt stress searches precede reduced consumer spending and increased defaults, which hurt retail and discretionary companies."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "When aggregate weekly Google Trends volume for debt-stress keywords increases 35% or more from prior week's baseline",
      "exit": "After 10 trading days or when search volume falls back below 15% above 4-week moving average",
      "expected_frequency": "medium"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "monthly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 24,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "high",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Economic cycles drive debt stress searches regularly. Recessions, interest rate shocks, and unemployment spikes all trigger 30-50% spikes multiple times per year."
  },
  {
    "idea": "EV charger network contraction signals electric vehicle adoption slowdown",
    "family": "travel_mobility",
    "data": {
      "source": "OpenChargeMap weekly charger count snapshot by state/region through openchargemap adapter",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "weekly",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "Charger count declines (due to removal or non-maintenance) signal reduced EV demand forecasts and subsidy cliff fears. Net charger count drops 8%+ over 4 weeks trigger EV/tech bearishness.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bearish",
      "sector": "XLK",
      "rationale": "EV charging infrastructure maturity is a leading indicator for EV adoption. Contraction signals weakening demand and policy headwinds for EV manufacturers."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "When net active charger count (OpenChargeMap US total) declines 3% or more over 2 consecutive weeks AND prior month growth was positive",
      "exit": "After 12 trading days or when charger count growth returns to positive YoY change",
      "expected_frequency": "high"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "weekly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 18,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "medium",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Charger network fluctuates seasonally and with funding cycles. Stimulus cliffs, maintenance pauses, and demand shifts produce regular 2-5% weekly swings."
  },
  {
    "idea": "Airline load factor and capacity utilization collapse signals travel demand destruction",
    "family": "travel_mobility",
    "data": {
      "source": "BTS airline load factor and available seat miles via bts_airline_load_factor adapter, weekly aggregates",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "weekly",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "Airlines cut capacity and load factors fall when bookings weaken. A 4%+ drop in load factor over 2 weeks signals business/leisure travel decline, hitting hospitality, financials, and transportation.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bearish",
      "sector": "XLY",
      "rationale": "Declining load factors indicate reduced corporate travel spending and leisure trips, both core to discretionary consumer and hospitality revenue."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "When weekly load factor (all carriers aggregate) declines 4% or more from 4-week moving average",
      "exit": "After 8 trading days or when load factor recovers to within 1% of 4-week MA",
      "expected_frequency": "medium"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "monthly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 24,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "high",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Airline demand cycles turn seasonally and with economic shocks. Holiday weeks, recession signals, and fuel/macro events regularly produce 4-8% swings in load factor."
  },
  {
    "idea": "Port container volume surge signals import demand recovery and consumer spending rebound",
    "family": "freight_logistics",
    "data": {
      "source": "Port of Los Angeles, Port of Long Beach, and Port of Newark container throughput via port_container_volume adapter, weekly data",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "weekly",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "Container imports spike 8-12 weeks ahead of retail sales beats when retailers frontload inventory in advance of holiday or promotional seasons. Sustained volume surge is bullish for discretionary.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bullish",
      "sector": "XLY",
      "rationale": "Rising port container volumes signal retailers are confident enough to increase inventory, a leading indicator for sales growth and profit."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "When weekly aggregate container volume (LA + LB + Newark TEU) exceeds 8-week moving average by 10% or more",
      "exit": "After 12 trading days or when volume falls back to within 5% of 8-week MA",
      "expected_frequency": "medium"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "weekly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 24,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "medium",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Port cycles turn seasonally and with retail calendars. Back-to-school (Aug), holiday season (Sep-Oct), and seasonal demand shifts produce regular 10-15% volume swings multiple times per year."
  },
  {
    "idea": "News article count spike on financial services regulatory failures triggers banking sector selloff",
    "family": "attention_sentiment",
    "data": {
      "source": "RSS feed count for news articles mentioning 'bank regulation', 'compliance violation', 'federal reserve enforcement' via rss_count adapter, weekly aggregates",
      "type": "scrape",
      "frequency": "weekly",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "Regulatory enforcement news and compliance scandals cluster around specific institutions or industry-wide reviews. A 40%+ spike in weekly enforcement/violation articles signals sell-first sentiment in financials.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bearish",
      "sector": "XLF",
      "rationale": "Bank regulatory news drives short-term risk-off sentiment and increases long-term cost expectations. Enforcement news is processed quickly by markets."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "When weekly RSS article count for banking regulatory keywords increases 40% or more from prior week",
      "exit": "After 6 trading days or when article count reverts below 20% above baseline",
      "expected_frequency": "medium"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "monthly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 24,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "high",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Regulatory cycles produce multiple enforcement actions per quarter. Stress tests, compliance exams, and scandal-driven coverage create regular 30-60% spikes in article counts."
  },
  {
    "idea": "Rare earth element and lithium commodity price surge signals supply shock and inflation expectations lift",
    "family": "macro_input_pressure",
    "data": {
      "source": "Commodity futures prices for lithium carbonate (China) and rare earth rare earth (US) via price_only adapter using SPDR commodity ETFs (XLB, LIT proxies) or direct futures",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "daily",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "Lithium and rare earth futures spike on supply disruptions (mine shutdowns, export bans) or demand surges (EV/renewable buildout acceleration). 15%+ 5-day moves trigger inflation concerns and materials rallies.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bullish",
      "sector": "XLB",
      "rationale": "Lithium and rare earth cost spikes are passed through to battery and electronics manufacturers, raising input costs but supporting materials producer margins."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "When lithium or rare earth futures rise 12% or more over 3 trading days AND close above 20-day moving average",
      "exit": "After 8 trading days or when price falls back below 20-day MA",
      "expected_frequency": "high"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "weekly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 24,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "high",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Commodity markets are volatile and supply disruptions are frequent. Mine accidents, geopolitical events, and demand shifts produce 10-30% weekly moves multiple times per year."
  },
  {
    "idea": "Earthquake activity surge in oil-producing regions triggers energy sector volatility and hedging demand",
    "family": "local_economy_weirdness",
    "data": {
      "source": "USGS earthquake activity API filtered for magnitude 4.5+ in Texas (Permian), Oklahoma (oil/gas), California, and Alaska via earthquake_activity adapter, daily data",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "daily",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "Seismic activity clusters in oil/gas producing regions due to induced seismicity from drilling/injection. Magnitude 4.5-5.5 earthquakes disrupt production and trigger supply risk premiums in crude futures.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bullish",
      "sector": "XLE",
      "rationale": "Oil supply shocks from earthquake-induced production disruptions drive crude futures higher and benefit energy producers on risk premium."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "When 2 or more magnitude 4.5+ earthquakes occur in oil-producing regions (Permian, OK, CA) within 5 trading days AND WTI crude rises 3% same day",
      "exit": "After 7 trading days or when earthquake activity subsides and crude normalizes",
      "expected_frequency": "medium"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "weekly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 24,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "medium",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Induced seismicity clusters form regularly in Permian and Oklahoma. Multi-event swarms happen several times per year, especially in spring/summer. USGS data is real-time and reliable."
